mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management and tuning for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d CLI tool, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the latest CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create the Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster named 'llm-d-cluster' with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command deploys the autoscaler configuration.
> This command deploys monitoring tools for the llm-d deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/all.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes all resources defined in the llm-d deployment.
> This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This process includes draining nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/all.yaml

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain.
> This command applies the latest deployment configurations.
> This command verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the image is not available or the credentials are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure you have access to the container registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the llm-d deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resource limits in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there is an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust the configuration as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the application is running.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new accelerators and improved scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated APIs related to legacy scheduling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.5.0, ensure to update your configuration files to match the new API specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in the 'Running' state, indicating that llm-d is successfully deployed.
codeSnippets: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
